Either way the partition rule says assign to neither.

No independent dimension (position, honour, edition co-occurrence) ties this stint to the Llanellyn record specifically.

A 1915-1936 Nigeria Treasury career is a large leap from a bio whose only attested service is St. Vincent 1900-01; the only bridge is initials plus career-shape continuity through the other Caribbean stints, which are themselves unassigned. The out-of-case biography darrell_arthur-llewellyn_b1883 is gate-compatible and already linked elsewhere, so assigning here risks a conflicting double-assignment.

notes: All four stints plausibly form ONE continuous career: 2nd Landing Waiter, St Vincent/Windward Islands 1905-1910 -> Clerk, Governor's Office, Grenada 1911-12 -> Clerk, Governor's Office, Windward Islands 1913-15 -> Nigeria Treasury 1915-1936 (the Windward listing ends 1915 exactly when Nigeria begins). The bio (b. 1883, St. Vincent, appears in edition 1913 while the Windward stint is listed 1913-15 as Clerk, Governor's Office) fits that career well. The blocker is darrell_arthur-llewellyn_b1883: identical birth year, identical initials, and 'Llanellyn'/'Llewellyn' differ by an OCR-plausible substitution -- these two biography records are very likely the same printed person duplicated by OCR, and that record is reported as already linked elsewhere. Recommended action: dedupe the two bio records at the biography level first; once merged, all four stints can be assigned to the canonical record with high confidence as a single coherent career. duplicate_bio_groups is left empty only because the Llewellyn bio is outside this case; reviewers should treat ['darrell_arthur-llanellyn_b1883', 'darrell_arthur-llewellyn_b1883'] as the de-facto duplicate pair.
